title: Apartments for Rent in Mentor, OH
metaTitle: Apartments for Rent in Mentor, Ohio
metaDescription: Searching for apartments for rent in Mentor, Ohio? See where Mentor sits relative to Cleveland, rent benchmarks, and how to tell our team what you need.
answerBox: Mentor, Ohio is the largest city in Lake County, roughly 25 miles northeast of downtown Cleveland. Rent Finder Cleveland's current selection is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, not Lake County, so tell us what you're looking for and we'll let you know what's available across Greater Cleveland.

sections.h2: Apartments for rent in Mentor, Ohio
body: Mentor sits in **Lake County**, about 25 miles northeast of downtown Cleveland, and it's the largest city in that county by population. That distance matters for renters comparing options: Mentor is a separate county from Cuyahoga, well outside the Cleveland city limits, and outside the area where our team currently works with rental homes.

**Rent Finder Cleveland's 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a few properties in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria — Mentor is not part of our current selection.** If Mentor apartments specifically are a must, a Lake County-focused property manager or local listing service will have more direct inventory there. If you're open to nearby Cleveland-area neighborhoods instead, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedroom count, budget, and whether you hold a housing voucher — and we'll point you to comparable options we do work with.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent an apartment near Cleveland?
body: Mentor-specific apartment rents will depend on the building and neighborhood, but as a general Greater Cleveland-area benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the metro's median asking rent at **$1,250/month**, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report — which weights toward larger professionally-managed apartment buildings — shows a higher metro average of **$1,564/month**. Both are useful reference points when comparing a Mentor listing to what similar apartments cost closer to Cleveland proper.

Mentor is in the northeast corner of the region, in Lake County rather than Cuyahoga County, which means it falls outside the coverage area of the Greater Cleveland Regional Transit Authority (GCRTA) rail and bus network that serves Cleveland and its Cuyahoga County suburbs. Renters relying on RTA for a downtown commute should factor in that a Mentor address means a longer drive rather than a direct rail or bus connection into the city.

Lake County, including Mentor, also sits within Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt, along with Geauga and Ashtabula counties and the eastern half of Cuyahoga County — an elevation and lake-proximity pattern that typically brings more seasonal snowfall than west-side, lakeshore-adjacent areas of Cuyahoga County see in a given winter.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers and apartments in Mentor
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8); acceptance is up to the individual property owner unless a specific municipality has passed its own protection. Mentor is not among the handful of Northeast Ohio municipalities reported to have a source-of-income protection ordinance, so voucher holders should confirm acceptance directly with any Mentor landlord or building.

What we can say with certainty is about our own homes: **every rental Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, though that inventory sits in Cleveland and a few nearby Ohio cities rather than Mentor. The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the local voucher program for Cuyahoga County and accepts preliminary applications year-round; Lake County renters would instead work with that county's housing authority. Our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers how the voucher process generally works in the Cleveland area.

If your apartment search includes Cleveland proper or nearby Lake County cities, our [apartments for rent in Cleveland](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the neighborhoods where we do work with rental homes. You can also compare notes on nearby Lake County communities like [Willoughby](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-willoughby-ohio/) and [Eastlake](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-eastlake-ohio/), or browse our full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) to see everywhere we cover.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland work with apartments in Mentor, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our current selection of 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a few properties in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. Mentor, in Lake County, is outside our current service area. Tell us what you need and we can point you to comparable options we do work with.

faq.q: How far is Mentor, Ohio from Cleveland?
faq.a: Mentor is roughly 25 miles northeast of downtown Cleveland, in Lake County. It's the largest city in that county and sits outside both Cuyahoga County and the GCRTA public transit network that serves Cleveland proper.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent an apartment near Cleveland?
faq.a: As a Greater Cleveland metro benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a median around $1,195 for a 1-bedroom and $1,100 for a 2-bedroom, while RentCafe's July 2026 report shows higher averages for larger professionally-managed buildings. Mentor-specific rents will vary by building.

faq.q: Do Mentor apartments accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord or building — Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance, and Mentor is not reported to have a local source-of-income protection ordinance. Confirm directly with any specific property. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts vouchers, though our inventory is in Cleveland rather than Mentor.

faq.q: Is Mentor served by Cleveland's RTA transit system?
faq.a: No. Mentor is in Lake County, outside the Greater Cleveland Regional Transit Authority's Cuyahoga County service area, so renters there typically rely on driving rather than RTA rail or bus routes for a Cleveland commute.
